key_insight: The Large Cancer Assistant (LCA) is a model-agnostic orchestration framework that separates cancer data routing from AI inference to make clinical decision support more scalable and modular.
clinical_relevance: This architecture could help future oncology decision-support systems integrate multimodal patient data more flexibly across different AI models and hospital IT environments.
method: Formalized LCA as a 7-tuple, post-hoc orchestration architecture based on algorithmic impermeability.
Used an Entry Theory with geometric deep learning to standardize multimodal patient data along structural and medical axes.
Routed data through a Cancer Switching Module and produced a Standardized Intermediate Payload (SIP) for downstream AI execution.
evidence_signal: A proof-of-concept tested four technical scenarios and reported negligible orchestration overhead, invariant routing during model swaps, and 100% recall for targeted supplementary data requests under injected anomalies.
watch_item: The framework was validated as a technical proof-of-concept, so real-world clinical robustness, EMR integration, and workflow impact still need prospective evaluation.
paper_title: The Large Cancer Assistant (LCA): A Model-Agnostic Orchestration Framework for Scalable Clinical Decision Support in Oncology
